description: Example targeted threat scenarios with threat intelligence for AI Threat Modeler

examples.name: APT29 Cozy Bear Campaign
examples.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1055 (Process Injection), T1071.001 (Web Protocols)
examples.vulnerabilities: CVE-2023-23397 (Outlook), CVE-2023-28252 (Windows CLFS), unpatched Exchange servers
examples.focusAreas: Email systems, Active Directory, cloud infrastructure
examples.scenarioDescription: APT29 targeting government agency through spearphishing campaign leading to AD compromise

threatIntelligence.rawIntelligence: CISA Alert AA23-347A: APT29 has been observed exploiting CVE-2023-23397 in Microsoft Outlook for initial access. The group uses WellMess malware and custom PowerShell scripts for persistence. Recent campaigns show focus on cloud environments, particularly Azure AD and M365.
threatIntelligence.recentCVEs:
CVE-2023-23397 (Outlook Elevation of Privilege)
CVE-2023-28252 (Windows CLFS Privilege Escalation)
CVE-2023-36884 (Office and Windows HTML RCE)
threatIntelligence.knownIOCs:
C2 Domains: microsofthelpcenter.org, windows-update-service.net
IP Addresses: 185.220.101.35, 185.220.101.37
File Hash (SHA256): 2f7df0e7ae8b0e5c5f5d5c5a9f9e8d7c6b5a4a3
threatIntelligence.campaignInfo: Operation 'SolarWinds' follow-up campaigns targeting supply chain and cloud infrastructure. Using legitimate cloud services for C2 communication.

examples.name: Lazarus Financial Heist
examples.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing App), T1055.012 (Process Hollowing), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)
examples.vulnerabilities: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228), unpatched Apache Struts, weak API authentication
examples.focusAreas: Web applications, API endpoints, database servers, payment processing systems
examples.scenarioDescription: Lazarus targeting financial institution's web infrastructure for monetary theft

threatIntelligence.rawIntelligence: FBI Flash Report: Lazarus Group actively exploiting Log4j vulnerabilities in financial sector. Using FASTCash malware variant for ATM cashouts. Cryptocurrency exchanges remain primary targets.
threatIntelligence.recentCVEs:
CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell)
CVE-2024-3094 (XZ Utils backdoor)
CVE-2023-34362 (MOVEit Transfer SQLi)
threatIntelligence.knownIOCs:
Bitcoin addresses: 12t9YDPgwueZ9NyMgw519p7AA8isjr6SMw
Malware samples: PowerRatankba.exe (MD5: a64a6e797917b0e9b0c6f62b983b9868)
C2 servers: update-microsoft.net:443
threatIntelligence.campaignInfo: Operation 'DreamJob' - fake job offers targeting cryptocurrency and fintech employees. Custom ransomware deployment after data exfiltration.

examples.name: APT28 Critical Infrastructure Attack
examples.ttps: T1133 (External Remote Services), T1021.001 (RDP), T1087 (Account Discovery), T1003 (Credential Dumping)
examples.vulnerabilities: Exposed RDP services, default credentials on SCADA systems, CVE-2024-1234 (fictional ICS vulnerability)
examples.focusAreas: SCADA networks, HMI interfaces, engineering workstations, OT/IT boundaries
examples.scenarioDescription: APT28 targeting energy sector industrial control systems through IT/OT convergence points

threatIntelligence.rawIntelligence: DHS ICS-CERT: APT28 observed targeting energy sector ICS environments. Using VPNFilter malware stage 3 modules for SCADA reconnaissance. Living-off-the-land techniques in OT networks.
threatIntelligence.recentCVEs:
CVE-2023-1234 (Schneider Electric Unity Pro)
CVE-2023-5678 (Siemens SIMATIC S7)
CVE-2024-9012 (ABB System 800xA)
threatIntelligence.knownIOCs:
Modbus scanner tool: ics-recon.exe
Rogue engineering workstation: 192.168.1.150
Malicious ladder logic: mal_plc_logic.st1
threatIntelligence.campaignInfo: Operation 'Dragonfly 2.0' continuation. Pre-positioning for potential disruptive attacks. Focus on gathering ICS network diagrams and process documentation.

examples.name: Ransomware Affiliate Campaign
examples.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1489 (Service Stop), T1083 (File Discovery)
examples.vulnerabilities: Unpatched VPN appliances, ProxyNotShell, weak MFA implementation
examples.focusAreas: Backup systems, domain controllers, file servers, VMware infrastructure
examples.scenarioDescription: Ransomware group targeting healthcare organization through vulnerable edge devices

threatIntelligence.rawIntelligence: HC3 Threat Brief: BlackCat ransomware affiliates increasingly targeting healthcare. Using stolen credentials from initial access brokers. ESXi servers primary target for maximum impact.
threatIntelligence.knownIOCs:
Ransomware executable: blackcat.exe (SHA256: 3a7e3f4b9c2d1e5f6a8b9c0d2e4f5a6b7c8d9e0f)
TOR sites: blackcatXXXXXXXX.onion
Exfiltration tool: rclone.exe renamed as svchost.exe
threatIntelligence.campaignInfo: Triple extortion model: encryption + data theft + DDoS threats. Average ransom demand $2.5M. 7-day countdown timer before data leak.

usage_notes.description: These examples can be used to test the targeted threat analysis feature with realistic threat intelligence

fields.rawIntelligence: Paste threat bulletins, CISA alerts, or security vendor reports
fields.recentCVEs: List relevant CVEs with brief descriptions
fields.knownIOCs: Include domains, IPs, file hashes, or other indicators
fields.campaignInfo: Describe ongoing campaigns, TTPs evolution, or targeting patterns
fields.intelligenceDate: When the intelligence was gathered or last updated
